---
title: "Consistency_bulk_vs_sc_RNAseq"
author: "Pakorn Sagulkoo"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
---

This code we will evaluate the consistency of gene expression and functional enrichment analysis from our tumour integrated results and bulk transcriptomic data from both human and mouse studies. For human bulk transcriptomes, we used the data from [Nadeu et al. (2022)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-022-01927-8) and [Parry et al. (2023a)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-022-02113-6) while for mouse bulk transcriptomes, the data came from [Hing et al. (2023)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-022-35778-1), [Ten Hacken et. al. (2023)](https://aacrjournals.org/bloodcancerdiscov/article/4/2/150/718388/In-Vivo-Modeling-of-CLL-Transformation-to-Richter), [Iyer et al. (2024)](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/scitranslmed.adg7915), and [Rompietti et al. (2025)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41375-025-02557-y)

# Import the relevant packages and set working path directory

```{r, setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Import the relavant packages
library(UpSetR)
library(ggvenn)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(lsa)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)
```

```{r}
# Check root path
here::here()
```

# Define paths

```{r}
# Define paths to load and save files
path_to_bulk_analysis <- here::here("RNAseq/systematic_review")
path_to_single_cell_analysis <- here::here("scRNAseq/systematic_review")
path_to_sc_integrated_data <- here::here("scRNAseq/meta_analysis/R_objects/gene_exprs")
path_to_save_figure <- here::here("figures")
```

# Load data and functions

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Source functions
source(here::here("bin/utils.R"))
```

```{r}
# List file names of all studies
study.lst <- c("1-Hacken_2023_GSE186137", "1-Nadeu_2022", "2-Iyer_2024_GSE263238", "2-Parry_2023a", 
               "3-Rompietti_2025", "5-Hing_2023_GSE183427")
study.lst
```

```{r}
# Create names for list later
names.lst <- sub(
  "^\\d+-([A-Za-z]+)_([0-9]{4}[A-Za-z]?).*$",  # capture name and year
  "\\1(\\2)",                         # output as Name(Year)
  study.lst
)

names.lst[length(names.lst) + 1] <- "Integrated scRNA-seq"
names.lst
```

```{r}
# Extract only name of studies
study_names <- sub("^\\d+-([^_0-9]+).*", "\\1", study.lst)
study_names
```

## Data from gene expression using DESeq2

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load expression results in each study and collect in a list
path_to_dea_download <- str_c(path_to_bulk_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/expression_data/", study_names, "_dea.rds")
path_to_dea_download[3] <- "/mnt/hp-storage/users/pakorns/RNAseq/systematic_review/2-Iyer_2024_GSE263238/expression_data/Iyer_RT_vs_CLL_dea.rds" # Iyer was used in different file name
path_to_dea_download[6] <- "/mnt/hp-storage/users/pakorns/RNAseq/systematic_review/5-Hing_2023_GSE183427/expression_data/Hing_GSE183427_dea.rds" # Hing was used in different file name
path_to_dea_download[length(path_to_dea_download) + 1] <- str_c(path_to_sc_integrated_data, "/tumour_deseq2_dea.rds") # add path for integrated dea

dea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_dea_download), 
  function(path){
    expr.mat <- readRDS(path)
    return(expr.mat)
  }
)

names(dea.lst) <- names.lst

# Convert some DESeq results to data frames
dea.lst$`Nadeu(2022)` <- as.data.frame(dea.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`)
dea.lst$`Parry(2023a)` <- as.data.frame(dea.lst$`Parry(2023a)`)
```

```{r}
# Clean the dea especially for column gene names to be consistent across all studies
names(dea.lst$`Hacken(2023)`)[names(dea.lst$`Hacken(2023)`) == "Gene.name"] <- "gene_name"
names(dea.lst$`Iyer(2024)`)[names(dea.lst$`Iyer(2024)`) == "Gene.name"] <- "gene_name"
names(dea.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`)[names(dea.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`) == "Gene.name"] <- "gene_name"
names(dea.lst$`Hing(2023)`)[names(dea.lst$`Hing(2023)`) == "Gene.name"] <- "gene_name"
dea.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`$gene_name <- rownames(dea.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`)
```

### Upregulated DEGs list

```{r}
# Extract adjusted significant upregulated genes and collect in a list
sig_upreg_gene.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(dea.lst),
  function(dea){
    dea <- dea[!is.na(dea$padj), ] # remove genes with padj = NA
    selected_gene <- dea[dea$log2FoldChange > 0.25 & 
                         dea$padj < 0.05 & 
                         !is.na(dea$gene_name) & 
                         dea$gene_name != "", "gene_name"]
    return(selected_gene)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
sig_upreg_gene.lst <- sig_upreg_gene.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 
```

### Downregulated DEGs list

```{r}
# Extract adjusted significant downregulated genes and collect in a list
sig_downreg_gene.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(dea.lst),
  function(dea){
    dea <- dea[!is.na(dea$padj), ] # remove genes with padj = NA
    selected_gene <- dea[dea$log2FoldChange < (-0.25) & 
                         dea$padj < 0.05 & 
                         !is.na(dea$gene_name) & 
                         dea$gene_name != "", "gene_name"]
    return(selected_gene)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
sig_downreg_gene.lst <- sig_downreg_gene.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 
```

## Data from GSEA

```{r}
# Hallmark
path_to_gsea_download <- str_c(path_to_bulk_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/enrichment_data/", study_names)
path_to_gsea_download[6] <- "/mnt/hp-storage/users/pakorns/RNAseq/systematic_review/5-Hing_2023_GSE183427/enrichment_data/Hing_GSE183427" # Hing was used in different file name
path_to_gsea_download[length(path_to_gsea_download) + 1] <- str_c(path_to_sc_integrated_data, "/tumour_deseq2") # add path for integrated gsea
path_to_hm <- str_c(path_to_gsea_download, "_hm_gsea.rds")
  
hm_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_hm), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)

names(hm_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

# GO:BP
path_to_go <- str_c(path_to_gsea_download, "_go_gsea.rds")
  
go_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_go), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)

names(go_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

# KEGG
path_to_kegg <- str_c(path_to_gsea_download, "_kegg_gsea.rds")
  
kegg_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_kegg), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)

names(kegg_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

# Reactome
path_to_reactome <- str_c(path_to_gsea_download, "_reactome_gsea.rds")
  
reactome_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_reactome), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)

names(reactome_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

# WikiPath
path_to_wikipath <- str_c(path_to_gsea_download, "_wikipath_gsea.rds")
  
wikipath_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_wikipath), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)

names(wikipath_gsea.lst) <- names.lst
```

# Mouse bulk RNA-seq consistency analysis

This section we evaluated the consistency of gene expression in mouse data sets.

## Direction consistency of the known DEGs

In this method, we showed the consistecy of expression direction of the known DEGs previously reported from the literature.

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract all expressed genes without considering significance 
mouse_studies <- c("Hacken(2023)", "Iyer(2024)", "Hing(2023)", "Rompietti(2025)")

common_mouse_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[mouse_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Mouse.gene.name`)) %>%
    pull(`Mouse.gene.name`)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

common_mouse_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(`Mouse.gene.name` %in% common_mouse_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(`Mouse.gene.name`, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
common_mouse_gene_expr.df <- common_mouse_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange
  ) 

DT::datatable(common_mouse_gene_expr.df, options = list(scrollX = TRUE))
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract all genes found in all study
all_mouse_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::select(`Mouse.gene.name`, gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
all_mouse_gene_expr.df <- all_mouse_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = list(log2FoldChange = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)) 
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Hacken_direction = dplyr::case_when(
      `Hacken(2023)` > 0 ~ "Up",
      `Hacken(2023)` < 0 ~ "Down",
      TRUE ~ "NA"
    ),
    Iyer_direction = dplyr::case_when(
      `Iyer(2024)` > 0 ~ "Up",
      `Iyer(2024)` < 0 ~ "Down",
      TRUE ~ "NA"
    ),
    Hing_direction = dplyr::case_when(
      `Hing(2023)` > 0 ~ "Up",
      `Hing(2023)` < 0 ~ "Down",
      TRUE ~ "NA"
    ),
    Rompietti_direction = dplyr::case_when(
      `Rompietti(2025)` > 0 ~ "Up",
      `Rompietti(2025)` < 0 ~ "Down",
      TRUE ~ "NA"
    )
  ) 

DT::datatable(all_mouse_gene_expr.df, options = list(scrollX = TRUE))
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Save the results
saveRDS(common_mouse_gene_expr.df, here::here("scRNAseq/meta_analysis/R_objects/common_mouse_gene_expr.rds"))
saveRDS(all_mouse_gene_expr.df, here::here("scRNAseq/meta_analysis/R_objects/all_mouse_gene_expr.rds"))
```

```{r}
# Define known mouse up- and downregulated DEGs
gene_mouse_categories <- list(
  # Upregulated DEGs 
  Cell_Cycle_Up = c("Mki67", "Cdk1", "Cdk6", "Ccna2", "Ccnb1", "Ccnd2", "Ccnd3"),
  Mitosis_DNA_Up = c("Aurkb", "Top2a"),
  Oncogenes_TF_Up = c("Myc", "Egr1"),
  Immune_Checkpoint_Up = c("Ctla4", "Cd274", "Il10"), # Immunosuppression and T-cell exhaustion markers (common in RT microenvironment)
  Microenv_Homing_Up = c("Cxcr5", "Ccr7", "Ccl17", "Ccl22", "Cx3cl1"), # Chemokines and Receptors for cell trafficking and interaction
  Signaling_Metabolism_Up = c("Ldha", "Tgfbr1", "Il16ra"),

  # Downregulated DEGs 
  Cycle_Inhibitors_Down = c("Cdkn1a", "Cdkn2b"), # Loss of cell cycle "brakes" (Tumor Suppressors)
  Oncogenes_TF_Down = c("Sox4", "Runx3", "Bcl6", "Bcl11b"),
  Immune_Checkpoint_Down = c("Cd74"),
  Microenv_Homing_Down = c("Itgb7"),
  Signaling_Metabolism_Down = c("Itk"),
  Immune_Reg_Down = c("Lair1") # Loss of inhibitory immune receptors
)

known_mouse_degs <- unlist(gene_mouse_categories)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=8, fig.width=2}
# Evaluate the consistency through the direction of the known DEGs by plotting the heatmap
desired_order <- c("Hing(2023)", "Hacken(2023)", "Iyer(2024)", "Rompietti(2025)")

# Extract expression matrix 
expr_mat <- as.matrix(common_mouse_gene_expr.df[ , -1])   # all columns except gene
rownames(expr_mat) <- common_mouse_gene_expr.df$`Mouse.gene.name`
valid_genes <- intersect(known_mouse_degs, rownames(expr_mat))
known_degs_expr_mat <- expr_mat[valid_genes, ]
known_degs_expr_mat <- apply(known_degs_expr_mat, 2, as.numeric)
rownames(known_degs_expr_mat) <- valid_genes
known_degs_expr_mat <- known_degs_expr_mat[ , desired_order]
colnames(known_degs_expr_mat) <- c("Hing(2023)", "Ten Hacken(2023)", "Iyer(2024)", "Rompietti(2025)")

# Set scale for heatmap
palette_length <- 100
colour_pal <- colorRampPalette(c("#053061", "#2171B5", "white", "#FFD92F", "#A50F15"))(palette_length)
value_limit <- max(abs(known_degs_expr_mat), na.rm = TRUE)
breaks <- seq(-value_limit, value_limit, length.out = palette_length + 1)

# Plot the heatmap
pheatmap(
  known_degs_expr_mat,
  scale = "none",
  color = colour_pal,
  breaks = breaks,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE
)
```

## Common upregulated DEGs

```{r}
# Select mouse data sets and extract adjusted significant upregulated genes and collect in a list
mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(dea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(dea){
    dea <- dea[!is.na(dea$padj), ] # remove genes with padj = NA
    selected_gene <- dea[dea$log2FoldChange > 0.25 & 
                         dea$padj < 0.05 & 
                         !is.na(dea$`Mouse.gene.name`) & 
                         dea$`Mouse.gene.name` != "", "Mouse.gene.name"]
    return(selected_gene)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst <- mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 
```

```{r}
# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_up_degs_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_up_degs_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated DEGs
mouse_common_up_degs <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_upreg_gene.lst)
mouse_common_up_degs
```

## Common downregulated DEGs

```{r}
# Select mouse data sets and extract adjusted significant downregulated genes and collect in a list
mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(dea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(dea){
    dea <- dea[!is.na(dea$padj), ] # remove genes with padj = NA
    selected_gene <- dea[dea$log2FoldChange < (-0.25) & 
                         dea$padj < 0.05 & 
                         !is.na(dea$`Mouse.gene.name`) & 
                         dea$`Mouse.gene.name` != "", "Mouse.gene.name"]
    return(selected_gene)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst <- mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 
```

```{r}
# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_down_degs_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_down_degs_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated DEGs
mouse_common_down_degs <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_downreg_gene.lst)
mouse_common_down_degs
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Save the results in the csv files
# 1. Pad the lists to the same length with NAs
max_len <- max(length(mouse_common_up_degs), length(mouse_common_down_degs))
length(mouse_common_up_degs) <- max_len 
length(mouse_common_down_degs) <- max_len

# 2. Combine into a Data Frame and set your Column Names here
mouse_common_degs <- data.frame(
  "Common_up_DEGs" = mouse_common_up_degs,   
  "Common_down_DEGs" = mouse_common_down_degs
)

# 3. Save as CSV
write.csv(
  mouse_common_degs, 
  file = here::here("scRNAseq/meta_analysis/supplm_file/mouse_bulk_common_degs.csv"), 
  row.names = FALSE,   # Removes the row numbers 
  na = ""              # Makes empty cells blank instead of writing "NA"
)
```

```{r, fig.height=2.5, fig.width=11}
# Visualise the heatmap for common up- and downregulated DEGs

# 1. Extract common DEGs expression data
mouse_common_degs <- c(mouse_common_up_degs, mouse_common_down_degs)
mouse_common_degs_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(`Mouse.gene.name` %in% mouse_common_degs) %>%
      dplyr::select(`Mouse.gene.name`, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# 2. Change the data frame to wider form and convert to a matrix
mouse_common_degs_expr.df <- mouse_common_degs_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(`Mouse.gene.name` = factor(`Mouse.gene.name`, levels = mouse_common_degs)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(`Mouse.gene.name`) %>%
  dplyr::rename("Ten Hacken(2023)" = `Hacken(2023)`) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    `Mouse.gene.name`, 
    `Hing(2023)`, 
    `Ten Hacken(2023)`, 
    `Iyer(2024)`, 
    `Rompietti(2025)`
  )

mouse_common_sig_expr_mat <- mouse_common_degs_expr.df %>% tibble::column_to_rownames(var = "Mouse.gene.name") %>% as.matrix()
mouse_common_sig_expr_mat <- t(mouse_common_sig_expr_mat)

# 4. Plot the heatmap
breaks <- seq(-10, 10, length.out = 101)
colour_pal <- colorRampPalette(c("#053061", "#2171B5", "white", "#FFD92F", "#A50F15"))(100)
pheatmap(
  mouse_common_sig_expr_mat,
  scale = "none",
  color = colour_pal,
  breaks = breaks,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE
)
```

## Common GSEA terms

### Hallmark

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(hm_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_up_hm_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_up_hm_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
mouse_common_up_hm <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_up_hm
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(hm_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_down_hm_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_down_hm_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
mouse_common_down_hm <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_down_hm
```

### GO:BP

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(go_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_up_go_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_up_go_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
mouse_common_up_go <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_up_go
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(go_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_down_go_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_down_go_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
mouse_common_down_go <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_down_go
```

### KEGG

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(kegg_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_up_kegg_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_up_kegg_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
mouse_common_up_kegg <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_up_kegg
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(kegg_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_down_kegg_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_down_kegg_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
mouse_common_down_kegg <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_down_kegg
```

### Reactome

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(reactome_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_up_reactome_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_up_reactome_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
mouse_common_up_reactome <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_up_reactome
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(reactome_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_down_reactome_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_down_reactome_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
mouse_common_down_reactome <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_down_reactome
```

### WikiPath

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(wikipath_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_up_wikipath_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_up_wikipath_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
mouse_common_up_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_up_wikipath
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
mouse_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(wikipath_gsea.lst[mouse_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
mouse_sig_term.lst <- mouse_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
mouse_down_wikipath_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Hing (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hing(2023)`,
       "Ten Hacken (2023)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Hacken(2023)`,
       "Iyer (2024)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Iyer(2024)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = mouse_sig_term.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899", "#F1788D"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

mouse_down_wikipath_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
mouse_common_down_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, mouse_sig_term.lst)
mouse_common_down_wikipath
```

## Numerical consistency analysis across gene expression

We also measured the gene expression consistency across those studies by selecting genes with significant expression to evaluate the correlation of those genes, see the following steps. We measured numerically consistency between the expression level through correlations and similarities.

### All significant genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract significant genes 
common_sig_mouse_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[mouse_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Mouse.gene.name`)) %>%
    pull(`Mouse.gene.name`)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

sig_mouse_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(`Mouse.gene.name` %in% common_sig_mouse_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(`Mouse.gene.name`, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
sig_mouse_gene_expr.df <- sig_mouse_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

# Rename and rearrange the column
sig_mouse_gene_expr.df <- sig_mouse_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::rename("Ten Hacken(2023)" = `Hacken(2023)`)

sig_mouse_gene_expr.df <- sig_mouse_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::select(
    `Mouse.gene.name`, 
    `Hing(2023)`, 
    `Ten Hacken(2023)`, 
    `Iyer(2024)`, 
    `Rompietti(2025)`
  )

head(sig_mouse_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- sig_mouse_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-`Mouse.gene.name`) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.4, fig.width=5.8}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Upregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract upregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_upreg_mouse_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[mouse_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange > 0.25) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Mouse.gene.name`)) %>%
    pull(`Mouse.gene.name`)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(`Mouse.gene.name` %in% common_upreg_mouse_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(`Mouse.gene.name`, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df <- mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

# Rename and rearrange the column
mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df <- mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::rename("Ten Hacken(2023)" = `Hacken(2023)`)

mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df <- mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::select(
    `Mouse.gene.name`, 
    `Hing(2023)`, 
    `Ten Hacken(2023)`, 
    `Iyer(2024)`, 
    `Rompietti(2025)`
  )

head(mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- mouse_upreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-`Mouse.gene.name`) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.4, fig.width=5.8}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Downregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract downregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_downreg_mouse_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[mouse_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange < (-0.25)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Mouse.gene.name`)) %>%
    pull(`Mouse.gene.name`)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(`Mouse.gene.name` %in% common_downreg_mouse_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(`Mouse.gene.name`, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df <- mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

# Rename and rearrange the column
mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df <- mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::rename("Ten Hacken(2023)" = `Hacken(2023)`)

mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df <- mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::select(
    `Mouse.gene.name`, 
    `Hing(2023)`, 
    `Ten Hacken(2023)`, 
    `Iyer(2024)`, 
    `Rompietti(2025)`
  )

head(mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- mouse_downreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-`Mouse.gene.name`) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.4, fig.width=5.8}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Upregulated GSEA

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Hallmark 
mouse_common_up_hm_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- hm_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_up_hm) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# GO:BP 
mouse_common_up_go_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- go_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_up_go) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# KEGG 
mouse_common_up_kegg_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- kegg_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_up_kegg) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Reactome 
mouse_common_up_reactome_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- reactome_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_up_reactome) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# WikiPath 
mouse_common_up_wikipath_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- wikipath_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_up_wikipath) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Join the data frames using rbind()
mouse_common_up_terms <- bind_rows(mouse_common_up_hm_nes.df, 
                                   mouse_common_up_go_nes.df, 
                                   mouse_common_up_kegg_nes.df,
                                   mouse_common_up_reactome_nes.df,
                                   mouse_common_up_wikipath_nes.df)
mouse_common_up_terms <- mouse_common_up_terms %>%
  dplyr::rename("Ten Hacken(2023)" = `Hacken(2023)`) %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(avrg_NES = mean(c_across(where(is.numeric)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ID,
    `Hing(2023)`, 
    `Ten Hacken(2023)`, 
    `Iyer(2024)`, 
    `Rompietti(2025)`,
    avrg_NES
  )

head(mouse_common_up_terms)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
gsea_mat <- mouse_common_up_terms %>% dplyr::select(-c("ID", "avrg_NES")) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(gsea_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(gsea_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.4, fig.width=5.8}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson Correlation (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman Correlation (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)
```

### Downregulated GSEA

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Hallmark 
mouse_common_down_hm_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- hm_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_down_hm) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# GO:BP 
mouse_common_down_go_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- go_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_down_go) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# KEGG 
mouse_common_down_kegg_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- kegg_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_down_kegg) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Reactome 
mouse_common_down_reactome_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- reactome_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_down_reactome) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# WikiPath 
mouse_common_down_wikipath_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  mouse_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- wikipath_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_down_wikipath) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Join the data frames using rbind()
mouse_common_down_terms <- bind_rows(mouse_common_down_hm_nes.df, 
                                   mouse_common_down_go_nes.df, 
                                   mouse_common_down_kegg_nes.df,
                                   mouse_common_down_reactome_nes.df,
                                   mouse_common_down_wikipath_nes.df)
mouse_common_down_terms <- mouse_common_down_terms %>%
  dplyr::rename("Ten Hacken(2023)" = `Hacken(2023)`) %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(avrg_NES = mean(c_across(where(is.numeric)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ID,
    `Hing(2023)`, 
    `Ten Hacken(2023)`, 
    `Iyer(2024)`, 
    `Rompietti(2025)`,
    avrg_NES
  )

head(mouse_common_down_terms)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
gsea_mat <- mouse_common_down_terms %>% dplyr::select(-c("ID", "avrg_NES")) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(gsea_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(gsea_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.4, fig.width=5.8}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson Correlation (downregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman Correlation (downregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (downregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (downregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)
```

## GSEA summarisation

```{r}
# Rbind both common up- and downregulated term tables
mouse_common_terms <- bind_rows(mouse_common_up_terms, mouse_common_down_terms)
DT::datatable(mouse_common_terms, options = list(scrollX = TRUE))
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Plot the bar graph
selected_terms <- c("HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS", "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1", "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT", "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSLATION",
                    "KEGG_CELL_CYCLE", "WP_RETINOBLASTOMA_GENE_IN_CANCER", "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE", 
                    "HALLMARK_KRAS_SIGNALING_UP", "GOBP_IMMUNE_RESPONSE_REGULATING_SIGNALING_PATHWAY", 
                    "GOBP_TYPE_II_INTERFERON_PRODUCTION", "GOBP_REGULATION_OF_T_CELL_ACTIVATION", 
                    "REACTOME_CLASS_A_1_RHODOPSIN_LIKE_RECEPTORS")

selected_enrichment.df <- mouse_common_terms %>%
  dplyr::filter(ID %in% selected_terms) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, avrg_NES) 

selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("HALLMARK_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("GOBP_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("KEGG_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("REACTOME_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("WP_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("_V1", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("IMMUNE_RESPONSE_REGULATING_SIGNALING_PATHWAY", "IMMUNE_RESPONSE", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("_", " ", selected_enrichment.df$ID)

selected_enrichment.df$ID <- factor(selected_enrichment.df$ID, levels = selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$Direction <- "Up"
selected_enrichment.df$Direction[selected_enrichment.df$avrg_NES<0] <- "Down"

gsea_bar.plt <- ggplot(selected_enrichment.df, aes(x=ID, y=avrg_NES, fill=Direction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color="black", width = 0.75) +  
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, color = "black"),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 100, l = 65, unit = "pt"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0) + 
  ylab("Average NES") + xlab(NULL) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#357EBD", "#D43F3A"))

gsea_bar.plt
```

# Human bulk and integrated single-cell RNA-seq consistency analysis

This section we evaluated the consistency of gene expression in human bulk and integrated data sets.

## Common upregulated DEGs

```{r}
# Select human data sets and extract adjusted significant upregulated genes and collect in a list
human_studies <- setdiff(names(dea.lst), mouse_studies)

human_sig_upreg_gene.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(dea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(dea){
    dea <- dea[!is.na(dea$padj), ] # remove genes with padj = NA
    selected_gene <- dea[dea$log2FoldChange > 0.25 & 
                         dea$padj < 0.05 & 
                         !is.na(dea$gene_name) & 
                         dea$gene_name != "", "gene_name"]
    return(selected_gene)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_upreg_gene.lst <- human_sig_upreg_gene.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 
```

```{r}
# Plot the Venn diagram
human_up_degs_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_up_degs_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated DEGs
human_common_up_degs <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_upreg_gene.lst)
human_common_up_degs
```

## Common downregulated DEGs

```{r}
# Select human data sets and extract adjusted significant upregulated genes and collect in a list
human_sig_downreg_gene.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(dea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(dea){
    dea <- dea[!is.na(dea$padj), ] # remove genes with padj = NA
    selected_gene <- dea[dea$log2FoldChange < (-0.25) & 
                         dea$padj < 0.05 & 
                         !is.na(dea$gene_name) & 
                         dea$gene_name != "", "gene_name"]
    return(selected_gene)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_downreg_gene.lst <- human_sig_downreg_gene.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 
```

```{r}
# Plot the Venn diagram
human_down_degs_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_down_degs_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated DEGs
human_common_down_degs <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_downreg_gene.lst)
human_common_down_degs
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Save the results in the csv files
# 1. Pad the lists to the same length with NAs
max_len <- max(length(human_common_up_degs), length(human_common_down_degs))
length(human_common_up_degs) <- max_len 
length(human_common_down_degs) <- max_len

# 2. Combine into a Data Frame and set your Column Names here
human_bulk_integrated_common_degs <- data.frame(
  "Common_up_DEGs" = human_common_up_degs,   
  "Common_down_DEGs" = human_common_down_degs
)

# 3. Save as CSV
write.csv(
  human_bulk_integrated_common_degs, 
  file = here::here("scRNAseq/meta_analysis/supplm_file/human_bulk_integrated_common_degs.csv"), 
  row.names = FALSE,   # Removes the row numbers 
  na = ""              # Makes empty cells blank instead of writing "NA"
)
```

## Common GSEA terms

### Hallmark

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(hm_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_up_hm_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_up_hm_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
human_common_up_hm <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_up_hm
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(hm_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_down_hm_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_down_hm_venn.plt
```

### GO:BP

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(go_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_up_go_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_up_go_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
human_common_up_go <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_up_go
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(go_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_down_go_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_down_go_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
human_common_down_go <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_down_go
```

### KEGG

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(kegg_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_up_kegg_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_up_kegg_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
human_common_up_kegg <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_up_kegg
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(kegg_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_down_kegg_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_down_kegg_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
human_common_down_kegg <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_down_kegg
```

### Reactome

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(reactome_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_up_reactome_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_up_reactome_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
human_common_up_reactome <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_up_reactome
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(reactome_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_down_reactome_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_down_reactome_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
human_common_down_reactome <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_down_reactome
```

### WikiPath

```{r}
# Upregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(wikipath_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_up_wikipath_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_up_wikipath_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common upregulated terms
human_common_up_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_up_wikipath
```

```{r}
# Downregulated terms
human_sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(wikipath_gsea.lst[human_studies]),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Drop empty ones
human_sig_term.lst <- human_sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) 

# Plot the Venn diagram
human_down_wikipath_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Bulk Nadeu (2022)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Bulk Parry (2023a)" = human_sig_term.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Integrated scRNA-seq" = human_sig_term.lst$`Integrated scRNA-seq`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

human_down_wikipath_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Collect common downregulated terms
human_common_down_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, human_sig_term.lst)
human_common_down_wikipath
```

## Numerical consistency analysis across gene expression

We also measured the gene expression consistency across those studies by selecting genes with significant expression to evaluate the correlation of those genes, see the following steps. We measured numerically consistency between the expression level through correlations and similarities.

### All significant genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract significant genes 
common_sig_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

sig_human_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_sig_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
sig_human_gene_expr.df <- sig_human_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

# Rename the column
sig_human_gene_expr.df <- sig_human_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    "Bulk Nadeu(2022)" = `Nadeu(2022)`,
    "Bulk Parry(2023a)" = `Parry(2023a)`
    ) 

head(sig_human_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- sig_human_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Upregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract upregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_upreg_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange > 0.25) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_upreg_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

# Rename the column
human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    "Bulk Nadeu(2022)" = `Nadeu(2022)`,
    "Bulk Parry(2023a)" = `Parry(2023a)`
    ) 

head(human_upreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Downregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract downregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_downreg_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange < (-0.25)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_downreg_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

# Rename the column
human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    "Bulk Nadeu(2022)" = `Nadeu(2022)`,
    "Bulk Parry(2023a)" = `Parry(2023a)`
    ) 

head(human_downreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Upregulated GSEA

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Hallmark 
human_common_up_hm_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- hm_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_up_hm) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# GO:BP 
human_common_up_go_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- go_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_up_go) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# KEGG 
human_common_up_kegg_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- kegg_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_up_kegg) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Reactome 
human_common_up_reactome_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- reactome_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_up_reactome) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# WikiPath 
human_common_up_wikipath_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- wikipath_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% mouse_common_up_wikipath) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Join the data frames using rbind()
human_common_up_terms <- bind_rows(human_common_up_hm_nes.df, 
                                   human_common_up_go_nes.df, 
                                   human_common_up_kegg_nes.df,
                                   human_common_up_reactome_nes.df,
                                   human_common_up_wikipath_nes.df)

human_common_up_terms <- human_common_up_terms %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(avrg_NES = mean(c_across(where(is.numeric)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    "Bulk Nadeu(2022)"    = `Nadeu(2022)`,
    "Bulk Parry(2023a)"   = `Parry(2023a)`
  )

head(human_common_up_terms)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
gsea_mat <- human_common_up_terms %>% dplyr::select(-c("ID", "avrg_NES")) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(gsea_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(gsea_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson Correlation (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman Correlation (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (upregulated GSEA)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12
)
```

### Downregulated GSEA

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Hallmark --> no common downregulated term found

# GO:BP 
human_common_down_go_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- go_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_down_go) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# KEGG 
human_common_down_kegg_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- kegg_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_down_kegg) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# Reactome 
human_common_down_reactome_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- reactome_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_down_reactome) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)

# WikiPath 
human_common_down_wikipath_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- wikipath_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% human_common_down_wikipath) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = study, values_from = NES)
```
