
```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- sig_iyer_human_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

##### Upregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract upregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_upreg_iyer_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[iyer_human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange > 0.25) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

iyer_human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  iyer_human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_upreg_iyer_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
iyer_human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- iyer_human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

head(iyer_human_upreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- iyer_human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

##### Downregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract downregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_downreg_iyer_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[iyer_human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange < (-0.25)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

iyer_human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  iyer_human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_downreg_iyer_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
iyer_human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- iyer_human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

head(iyer_human_downreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- iyer_human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

### Rompietti (2025)

#### Gene overlapping analysis

```{r}
# Plot the Venn diagram
rompietti_human_up_degs_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Nadeu (2022)" = bulk_mouse_human_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Parry (2023a)" = bulk_mouse_human_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = bulk_mouse_human_sig_upreg_gene.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

rompietti_human_up_degs_venn.plt
```

```{r}
# Plot the Venn diagram
rompietti_human_down_degs_venn.plt <- ggvenn(
  list("Nadeu (2022)" = bulk_mouse_human_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Nadeu(2022)`,
       "Parry (2023a)" = bulk_mouse_human_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Parry(2023a)`,
       "Rompietti (2025)" = bulk_mouse_human_sig_downreg_gene.lst$`Rompietti(2025)`
       ),
  fill_color = c("#0073C2FF", "#EFC000FF", "#6FB899"),
  stroke_size = 0.5,
  set_name_size = 4.5,   # Size of the outside labels (Default is 6)
  text_size = 4        # Size of the numbers inside the diagram (Default is 4)
)

rompietti_human_down_degs_venn.plt
```

#### Numerical consistency analysis across gene expression

##### All significant genes

```{r}
# Set selected studies
rompietti_human_studies <- c("Rompietti(2025)", "Nadeu(2022)", "Parry(2023a)")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract significant genes 
common_sig_rompietti_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[rompietti_human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

sig_rompietti_human_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  rompietti_human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_sig_rompietti_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
sig_rompietti_human_gene_expr.df <- sig_rompietti_human_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

head(sig_rompietti_human_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- sig_rompietti_human_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (significant genes)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

##### Upregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract upregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_upreg_rompietti_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[rompietti_human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange > 0.25) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

rompietti_human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  rompietti_human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_upreg_rompietti_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
rompietti_human_upreg_gene_expr.df <- rompietti_human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

head(rompietti_human_upreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- rompietti_human_upreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (upregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

##### Downregulated genes

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Extract downregulated genes and their log2FC 
common_downreg_rompietti_human_genes <- purrr::map(
  dea.lst[rompietti_human_studies],
  ~ .x %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange < (-0.25)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_name)) %>%
    pull(gene_name)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

rompietti_human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  rompietti_human_studies,
  function(study_name){
    dea <- dea.lst[[study_name]]
    gene_expr <- dea %>%
      dplyr::filter(gene_name %in% common_downreg_rompietti_human_genes) %>%
      dplyr::select(gene_name, log2FoldChange) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gene_expr)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
rompietti_human_downreg_gene_expr.df <- rompietti_human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = log2FoldChange,
    values_fn = mean # Average duplicates to get a single number
  )

head(rompietti_human_downreg_gene_expr.df)
```

```{r}
# Remove the gene column for correlation
expr_mat <- rompietti_human_downreg_gene_expr.df %>% dplyr::select(-gene_name) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(expr_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(expr_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(expr_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
```

```{r, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=6.2}
# Plot with viridis colour palette
pheatmap(
  pearson_cor,
  main = "Pearson correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12         
)

pheatmap(
  spearman_cor,
  main = "Spearman correlation (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  cosine_similar,
  main = "Cosine similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)

pheatmap(
  euclidean_similar,
  main = "Euclidean similarity (downregulated DEGs)",
  color = viridis(100),
  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = length(viridis(100)) + 1),
  display_numbers = TRUE,
  number_color = "black",
  border_color = NA,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  fontsize_number = 12,
  fontsize_row = 12,        
  fontsize_col = 12 
)
```

# Session Information

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
